name: meeting-minder
goal: Build the Areté Meeting Minder — a local web app (arete view) that lets users sync, process, review, and approve meetings using AI-powered processing via the Pi SDK, with a React + Vite frontend imported from the meeting-minder Lovable prototype.

userStories.title: Package structure + Lovable import
userStories.description: Establish packages/apps/ directory structure. Import the meeting-minder Lovable prototype (https://github.com/johnkoht/meeting-minder) as packages/apps/web/. Create packages/apps/backend/ with correct package.json and tsconfig. Add convenience scripts to root package.json. Verify existing typecheck passes.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/apps/web/ contains the meeting-minder code and runs with npm run dev
packages/apps/backend/ exists with package.json (@arete/backend) and tsconfig (NodeNext, extends tsconfig.base.json)
Backend dependencies include: hono, @hono/node-server, @arete/core, @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent, gray-matter, yaml
Root package.json includes dev:web, dev:backend, build:apps convenience scripts
npm run typecheck passes across all packages with no new errors
No conflicts with existing packages/core/ or packages/cli/ tsconfigs

userStories.title: Meeting file data model + core utilities
userStories.description: Update MeetingForSave type and saveMeetingFile in packages/core to support status field and attendees-at-sync-time. Create packages/core/src/integrations/staged-items.ts with utilities for parsing and writing staged item sections and per-item status to meeting frontmatter.
acceptanceCriteria: MeetingForSave type includes optional status?: 'synced' | 'processed' | 'approved' field
saveMeetingFile writes status: synced and attendees array to frontmatter
staged-items.ts exports: generateItemId, parseStagedSections, parseStagedItemStatus, writeItemStatusToFile, commitApprovedItems
parseStagedSections correctly parses ## Staged Action Items, ## Staged Decisions, ## Staged Learnings sections with ai_NNN/de_NNN/le_NNN ID format
writeItemStatusToFile updates staged_item_status and staged_item_edits in frontmatter without corrupting the rest of the file
commitApprovedItems appends approved decisions to .arete/memory/items/decisions.md and learnings to .arete/memory/items/learnings.md, removes staged sections from body, sets status: approved
All utilities exported from packages/core/src/index.ts

packages/core/test/integrations/staged-items.test.ts covers: parseStagedSections (valid + malformed), parseStagedItemStatus (missing/partial frontmatter), writeItemStatusToFile round-trips, commitApprovedItems writes to memory and cleans file
npm run typecheck passes
Existing krisp and meeting integration tests still pass

userStories.title: Backend server + API endpoints
userStories.description: Build the Hono server in packages/apps/backend/src/ with all API endpoints. Uses @arete/core for file I/O. Reads ARETE_WORKSPACE env var for workspace root. Implements: meetings CRUD, sync job (async), process-people (sync shell-out), approve (commit staged items to memory), job polling. Does NOT implement Pi SDK processing yet (Task 4).
acceptanceCriteria: server.ts creates Hono app with CORS, JSON error handling, /health endpoint
GET /api/meetings returns array of meeting summaries (title, date, status, attendees, duration, source) from workspace resources/meetings/
GET /api/meetings/:slug returns full parsed meeting including staged items with their statuses from staged_item_status frontmatter
DELETE /api/meetings/:slug deletes the file and refreshes QMD index
PUT /api/meetings/:slug saves edits to summary/title to the markdown file
PATCH /api/meetings/:slug/items/:id updates staged_item_status and staged_item_edits in frontmatter (approve/skip/edit)
POST /api/meetings/:slug/approve calls commitApprovedItems from core, returns updated meeting
POST /api/meetings/sync shells out to arete pull krisp, returns { jobId } (202), job transitions running → done/error
GET /api/jobs/:id returns current job status { status, output }
POST /api/meetings/:slug/process-people shells out to arete meeting process --file <path> --json, returns result
POST /api/meetings/:slug/process returns { jobId } 202 (agent integration stubbed/TODO for Task 4)
GET /api/meetings/:slug/process-stream returns SSE stream (stubbed for Task 4)
If ARETE_WORKSPACE not set or not a valid workspace, server fails to start with clear error
packages/apps/backend/test/routes/meetings.test.ts covers all endpoints with mocked workspace service
npm run typecheck passes for backend package

userStories.title: Pi SDK agent integration for meeting processing
userStories.description: Implement the process endpoint and SSE stream using the Pi SDK. Backend creates an in-memory agent session with the workspace's process-meetings skill, sends the processing prompt, streams agent events to the client via SSE, and signals completion when the agent finishes.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/apps/backend/src/services/agent.ts exports createProcessingSession(workspaceRoot, meetingSlug) using createAgentSession from @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
Session uses: cwd = workspaceRoot, tools = createCodingTools(workspaceRoot), SessionManager.inMemory()
Prompt instructs agent to: use process-meetings skill, write staged sections with ai_NNN/de_NNN/le_NNN IDs, set status: processed, NOT write to memory
Agent events (text deltas, tool calls) are appended to job event log in real-time
GET /api/meetings/:slug/process-stream is a proper SSE endpoint that tails the job event log and closes when job is done/error
POST /api/meetings/:slug/process returns 503 with { error, hint } if Anthropic API key is not configured
Job transitions to done when agent_end event fires; job transitions to error on agent exception
Tests mock createAgentSession to emit predetermined events and verify job lifecycle transitions

userStories.title: Wire web app to backend API
userStories.description: Replace all mock data in packages/apps/web/ with real API calls using TanStack Query hooks. Handle all async states (loading, error, success). Implement live processing stream display. Wire all buttons and interactions to real endpoints. Add toast notifications.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/apps/web/src/api/client.ts provides base fetch wrapper; VITE_API_URL env var controls backend URL (defaults to http://localhost:3847)
packages/apps/web/src/api/meetings.ts exports typed functions for all backend endpoints
Hooks in src/hooks/: useMeetings, useMeeting, useApproveItem, useSaveApprove, useProcessPeople, useProcessMeeting, useSyncKrisp, useJobStatus
MeetingsIndex.tsx uses useMeetings(); shows loading skeleton and error state
Needs Attention section shows only synced/processed meetings with correct action buttons
All Meetings section has working client-side search filtering by title and attendee name
Sync Krisp button shows loading state during job, refreshes meeting list on completion
Process Meeting button opens live progress panel showing SSE stream output as it arrives
Processing indicator in Needs Attention table for active process jobs
MeetingDetail.tsx uses useMeeting(slug); per-item approve/skip/edit calls PATCH immediately
Save & Approve calls POST /approve and invalidates useMeeting + useMeetings queries
→ Next button navigates to next triage item; confirmation dialog appears when items have been reviewed but Save & Approve not yet clicked
After approve: green success banner + Next in Triage link shown
Toast notifications: sync complete, process complete, approve complete, all errors
No mock data from src/data/meetings.ts used in production components
packages/apps/web/src/test/ has hook tests with mocked fetch

userStories.title: Update process-meetings skill for staged output
userStories.description: Update packages/runtime/skills/process-meetings/SKILL.md to write staged items to the meeting file instead of immediately committing to .arete/memory/items/. Add --commit flag for backward compatibility with existing CLI users.
acceptanceCriteria: Default behavior (no --commit flag): skill writes ## Staged Action Items, ## Staged Decisions, ## Staged Learnings sections to the meeting file with ai_NNN/de_NNN/le_NNN IDs
Default behavior: skill sets status: processed and processed_at in meeting frontmatter
Default behavior: skill does NOT write to .arete/memory/items/ in staged mode
With --commit flag: existing behavior preserved — writes directly to memory, no staged sections
ID format is exactly: ai_001, de_001, le_001 (zero-padded to 3 digits, sequential within the meeting)
Section headers are exactly: ## Staged Action Items, ## Staged Decisions, ## Staged Learnings
Arguments section in SKILL.md documents --commit flag with description
SKILL.md includes a note: when run from arete view, staged mode is default; use --commit for CLI-only workflows
People/entity resolution behavior (attendee_ids, person file creation) unchanged

userStories.title: arete view CLI command
userStories.description: Add arete view command to packages/cli/ that resolves the workspace root, spawns the backend server with ARETE_WORKSPACE env var, waits for server ready, opens the browser, and handles graceful shutdown.
acceptanceCriteria: packages/cli/src/commands/view.ts registered in the CLI command index
Command resolves workspace root using services.workspace.findRoot(); errors with clear message if not in workspace
Port resolution: tries PORT env var, then 3847, 3848, 3849; errors clearly if all busy
Spawns backend server as child process with ARETE_WORKSPACE and PORT env vars
Waits for server ready by polling GET /health (max 5s, 500ms intervals); exits with error if server doesn't start
Opens browser using open package (or platform shell command) to http://localhost:{port}
Prints to stdout: Areté workspace open at http://localhost:{port} / Press Ctrl+C to stop.
SIGINT handler kills backend child process cleanly (no zombie processes)
Tests mock child_process.spawn and browser open; verify port conflict handling and SIGINT cleanup
npm run typecheck passes for cli package
